items.title: Nokia withdraws action against Geely in connected cars dispute
items.title_zh: 诺基亚撤回针对吉利车联网专利诉讼
items.title_en: Nokia Withdraws Munich Infringement Action Against Geely in Connected Car SEP Dispute
items.summary: As Munich Regional Court has now confirmed, Nokia has withdrawn a lawsuit against Geely that was due to be heard today.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地区法院确认，诺基亚已撤回原定于今日开庭的针对吉利的诉讼。该诉讼涉及用于联网汽车的4G和5G标准必要专利（EP 3 566 488和EP 3 832 976）。此举可能意味着包括统一专利法院（UPC）在内的进一步程序提前结束。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich Regional Court has confirmed that Nokia withdrew its patent infringement lawsuit against Geely, which was scheduled for an oral hearing on May 21, 2026. The action concerned two European patents essential to 4G and 5G standards used in connected vehicles (EP 3 566 488 and EP 3 832 976). This withdrawal halts the immediate proceedings in Munich and suggests that parallel litigation efforts, including those potentially pending at the Unified Patent Court (UPC), may also conclude prematurely without a substantive judgment on the merits.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案撤诉标志着新一轮车联网专利诉讼浪潮尚未全面展开即告一段落。实务中需关注诺基亚与吉利是否达成和解或许可协议，以及该撤诉对UPC相关并行案件的具体影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights the volatility of Standard Essential Patent (SEP) enforcement strategies in the automotive sector. For rights holders and implementers, the premature end of litigation underscores the importance of monitoring settlement dynamics and licensing negotiations outside of public court records. It serves as a reminder that high-profile SEP filings do not always proceed to full trial, influencing how parties assess litigation risk and portfolio valuation in cross-border disputes.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Munich Regional Court confirmed Nokia's withdrawal of the infringement claim against Geely prior to the scheduled oral hearing.", "The dispute centered on two standard-essential patents (EP 3 566 488 and EP 3 832 976) relevant to 4G and 5G connectivity in automobiles.", "The withdrawal indicates a potential early resolution of this specific enforcement wave, possibly affecting related UPC proceedings."]

The dispute centered on two standard-essential patents (EP 3 566 488 and EP 3 832 976) relevant to 4G and 5G connectivity in automobiles.
The withdrawal indicates a potential early resolution of this specific enforcement wave, possibly affecting related UPC proceedings.

Practice note: Monitor the status of any parallel actions at the Unified Patent Court, as national withdrawals often correlate with the discontinuation of multi-jurisdictional cam…

items.title: Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform
items.title_zh: 瑞士通过专利法修订引入实质审查制度
items.title_en: Switzerland Reforms Patent Act with Mandatory Searches and Optional Substantive Examination
items.summary: Key changes in the Swiss Patent Act include a mandatory prior-art search, optional full substantive examination, the acceptance of English-language technical documents and a new appeal system. In March 2024, the Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, introducing new features to the Swiss patent system. The Swiss government, as supported by […] The post Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 2024年3月，瑞士联邦议会通过《专利法》部分修订案。此次改革核心在于引入强制性的现有技术检索，并允许申请人选择是否进行全面的实质审查。此外，新规接受英文技术文件，并建立了新的上诉系统。这一变革标志着瑞士从形式审查向更严格的审查模式转变，旨在提升专利质量与法律确定性，对在该国进行专利布局的企业将产生深远影响。
items.ai_summary_en: In March 2024, the Swiss Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, introducing significant procedural changes. Key amendments include the implementation of a mandatory prior-art search and an optional full substantive examination system. The reform also allows for the acceptance of English-language technical documents and establishes a new appeal mechanism. These measures mark a shift towards enhanced patent quality and legal certainty within the Swiss jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业赴瑞士申请专利需适应新流程，强制检索可能增加前期成本与时间。建议提前规划现有技术分析，利用英文文件便利降低翻译成本。同时，关注实质审查的可选性，根据商业价值权衡投入，以应对更高的授权门槛和潜在的驳回风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This legislative update alters the procedural landscape for patent registration in Switzerland. Rights holders should note the introduction of mandatory searches and the option for substantive review, which may impact prosecution timelines and grant probabilities. The acceptance of English documentation offers potential administrative efficiencies for international applicants managing European portfolios.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["瑞士联邦议会于2024年3月通过《专利法》部分修订案", "引入强制性现有技术检索及可选的全面实质审查机制", "接受英文技术文档并建立全新的专利上诉系统"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The revised Swiss Patent Act introduces a mandatory prior-art search requirement for all patent applications filed in Switzerland.", "Applicants may now opt for a full substantive examination, moving beyond the previous formal examination framework.", "The reform permits the submission of English-language technical documents and implements a new appellate structure."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["适应强制检索要求，优化前期现有技术分析与预算规划", "善用英文文件政策降低成本，审慎评估实质审查的商业必要性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor how the optional substantive examination affects grant rates and portfolio strength in the Swiss market.", "Practice note: Leverage the new allowance for English-language technical documents to streamline filing procedures where applicable.", "What to monitor: Track the implementation timeline and specific guidelines for the newly established appeal system."]
core_points_en: The revised Swiss Patent Act introduces a mandatory prior-art search requirement for all patent applications filed in Switzerland.
Applicants may now opt for a full substantive examination, moving beyond the previous formal examination framework.
The reform permits the submission of English-language technical documents and implements a new appellate structure.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor how the optional substantive examination affects grant rates and portfolio strength in the Swiss market.
Practice note: Leverage the new allowance for English-language technical documents to streamline filing procedures where applicable.
What to monitor: Track the implementation timeline and specific guidelines for the newly established appeal system.

items.title: Nokia settles global 5G patent dispute with Chinese automaker Geely
items.title_zh: 诺基亚与中国车企吉利达成全球5G专利和解
items.title_en: Nokia Settles Global 5G Patent Dispute With Geely As Avanci Adds New Licensee
items.summary: UPDATE: Since we published our report, Avanci has also announced that it has signed 4G and 5G licences with one new unnamed Chinese car company, and Avanci licensor Longhorn IP has revealed that it has terminated a recently-launched German lawsuit against Geely.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，Avanci宣布与一家未具名中国车企签署4G和5G许可协议。同时，Avanci被许可人Longhorn IP透露，其已终止近期针对吉利在德国提起的诉讼。标题指出诺基亚已与吉利达成全球5G专利纠纷和解。
items.ai_summary_en: Nokia has settled its global 5G patent dispute with Chinese automaker Geely. Concurrently, the Avanci licensing platform announced new 4G and 5G licenses with an unnamed Chinese car manufacturer. Additionally, Avanci licensor Longhorn IP confirmed it terminated a recently filed lawsuit against Geely in Germany, signaling further resolution in ongoing automotive SEP licensing negotiations.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note: 需持续观察德国法院对已终止诉讼的最终程序裁定，以及Avanci平台新增许可对中国车企参与SEP许可池的市场信号意义。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights the continued consolidation of licensing frameworks for connected vehicles in Europe. The termination of parallel litigation alongside platform-based licensing suggests a preference for negotiated settlements among major SEP holders and automotive OEMs. Stakeholders should track how these bilateral and pool-based resolutions influence broader industry compliance trends.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题显示诺基亚已与中国汽车制造商吉利达成全球5G专利纠纷和解", "Avanci宣布与一家未具名的中国新汽车公司签署了4G和5G许可协议", "Avanci被许可人Longhorn IP终止了近期在德国针对吉利提起的诉讼"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Nokia reached a settlement resolving its global 5G standard essential patent dispute with Geely.", "Avanci secured new 4G and 5G licensing agreements with an additional unnamed Chinese vehicle manufacturer.", "Longhorn IP terminated its pending litigation against Geely within the German jurisdiction."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Demonstrates active resolution pathways for SEP disputes involving automotive manufacturers and major licensors.", "Practice note: Monitor the interplay between individual settlements and multi-pool licensing platforms like Avanci.", "What to monitor: Observe procedural outcomes of terminated cases and future licensing announcements from Avanci."]
core_points_en: Nokia reached a settlement resolving its global 5G standard essential patent dispute with Geely.
Avanci secured new 4G and 5G licensing agreements with an additional unnamed Chinese vehicle manufacturer.
Longhorn IP terminated its pending litigation against Geely within the German jurisdiction.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Demonstrates active resolution pathways for SEP disputes involving automotive manufacturers and major licensors.
Practice note: Monitor the interplay between individual settlements and multi-pool licensing platforms like Avanci.

items.title: FRAND battle between Huawei and TP-Link now focused at UK High Court
items.title_zh: 华为与TP-Link FRAND战焦点转向英国高等法院
items.title_en: Huawei Withdraws UPC and Munich Claims, Focuses FRAND Dispute with TP-Link on UK High Court
items.summary: Huawei has withdrawn its infringement claims against TP-Link at the UPC and Munich Regional Court and only the UK FRAND proceedings remain.
items.ai_summary_zh: 华为已撤回在统一专利法院（UPC）和慕尼黑地方法院对TP-Link的侵权索赔，争议焦点现集中于英国高等法院的FRAND费率设定程序。此前英国法官Richard Meade已裁定临时许可费率。华为于2025年夏季在UPC慕尼黑分部及慕尼黑地方法院提起诉讼，同时在中国法院起诉TP-Link。
items.ai_summary_en: Huawei has withdrawn its patent infringement claims against TP-Link at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and the Munich Regional Court. The multi-jurisdictional dispute concerning Wi-Fi 6 technology now centers exclusively on FRAND rate-setting proceedings before the UK High Court. This strategic shift follows a recent determination by Mr Justice Richard Meade regarding an interim licence rate. Huawei originally initiated parallel litigation in summer 2025 across the UPC's Munich local division, German national courts, and Chinese courts. The withdrawal of continental European infringement actions suggests a consolidation of efforts around the UK forum following the interim rate ruling.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案显示SEP权利人策略性调整诉讼阵地，利用英国法院确定的临时费率作为谈判杠杆，从而退出其他法域的侵权程序。实务中需关注英国FRAND判决对全球许可费率的示范效应，以及权利人在UPC撤案后是否会在成员国法院重启侵权主张以施压被诉方接受费率。
items.ai_insight_en: This development highlights the continued strategic importance of the UK High Court in global FRAND determinations. Rights holders may prioritize jurisdictions offering robust rate-setting mechanisms to establish licensing benchmarks, subsequently withdrawing parallel infringement actions in other regions once interim rates are secured. For implementers, this underscores the need to closely monitor UK judicial outcomes, as they often serve as reference points for global licensing negotiations and settlement discussions across multiple territories.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["华为撤回UPC及慕尼黑地方法院对TP-Link的侵权索赔，聚焦英国FRAND程序", "英国高等法院法官Richard Meade已确定涉案Wi-Fi 6技术的临时许可费率", "华为曾于2025年夏在UPC慕尼黑分部及中国法院对TP-Link提起平行诉讼"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Huawei withdrew infringement claims against TP-Link at the UPC and Munich Regional Court after UK interim rate setting.", "The dispute over Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents now focuses solely on FRAND proceedings at the UK High Court.", "Parallel litigation continues in Chinese courts, while European enforcement actions have been discontinued by Huawei."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: SEP实施人需评估英国FRAND裁定对…", "Practice note/实务观察: 权利人在获得有利费率裁定后撤回侵权诉讼是常见策略", "What to monitor/后续观察: 关注英国最终FRAND判决结果及华为在其他…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: SEP implementers should assess how UK interim rate rulings influence global negotiation leverage and potential settlement terms.", "Practice note: Rights holders may consolidate litigation strategies around forums providing definitive rate-setting capabilities after securing interim measures.", "What to monitor: The final FRAND judgment from the UK High Court and any subsequent enforcement or licensing activities in remaining jurisdictions like China."]
core_points_en: Huawei withdrew infringement claims against TP-Link at the UPC and Munich Regional Court after UK interim rate setting.
The dispute over Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents now focuses solely on FRAND proceedings at the UK High Court.
Parallel litigation continues in Chinese courts, while European enforcement actions have been discontinued by Huawei.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: SEP implementers should assess how UK interim rate rulings influence global negotiation leverage and potential settlement terms.
Practice note: Rights holders may consolidate litigation strategies around forums providing definitive rate-setting capabilities after securing interim measures.
What to monitor: The final FRAND judgment from the UK High Court and any subsequent enforcement or licensing activities in remaining jurisdictions like China.

items.title: UK court observes “evidentiary void” in withdrawn Qualcomm 'no licence, no chips' claim
items.title_zh: 英法院指出高通“无许可无芯片”索赔存在证据真空
items.title_en: UK Tribunal Approves Withdrawal of Qualcomm 'No Licence, No Chips' Claim Over Evidentiary Gaps
items.summary: The Competition Appeal Tribunal has approved the scrapping of a £480 million opt-out collective action claim, flagging an absence of witnesses from Apple and Samsung and lacking evidence of refusal to supply
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国竞争上诉法庭批准撤销一项4.8亿英镑的退出式集体诉讼索赔。法院指出该案存在“证据真空”，主要因为缺乏苹果和三星的证人出庭，且缺少拒绝供应的确凿证据。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Competition Appeal Tribunal has approved the discontinuance of a £480 million opt-out collective action claim against Qualcomm. The court highlighted a significant 'evidentiary void' in the case, specifically noting the absence of witness testimony from key industry players Apple and Samsung. Additionally, the tribunal found insufficient evidence to support allegations of refusal to supply, leading to the approval of the claim's withdrawal.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务观察：本案凸显了在涉及大型科技巨头的反垄断或SEP集体诉讼中，关键证人证言对于构建完整证据链的决定性作用。后续需关注此类因证据不足而撤诉的案件是否会影响其他类似诉讼的策略调整。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the critical importance of securing cooperation from major market participants in complex competition litigation. For rights holders and defendants alike, the inability to obtain testimony from key counterparties can create insurmountable evidentiary hurdles. Practitioners should monitor how tribunals assess evidentiary sufficiency in future collective actions involving standard-essential patents and supply chain disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["英国竞争上诉法庭批准撤销4.8亿英镑的退出式集体诉讼索赔", "法院认定案件存在“证据真空”，缺乏苹果和三星的证人及拒绝供应证据"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Competition Appeal Tribunal approved the scrapping of a £480 million opt-out collective action claim against Qualcomm.", "The court identified an evidentiary void due to the lack of witnesses from Apple and Samsung.", "Insufficient evidence regarding refusal to supply contributed to the decision to withdraw the claim."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Highlights risks in collective actions where key third-party witnesses are unavailable.", "Practice note: Demonstrates that tribunals will strictly scrutinize evidence of refusal to supply and market conduct.", "What to monitor: Future procedural approaches to evidentiary gaps in SEP-related competition claims."]
core_points_en: The Competition Appeal Tribunal approved the scrapping of a £480 million opt-out collective action claim against Qualcomm.
The court identified an evidentiary void due to the lack of witnesses from Apple and Samsung.
Insufficient evidence regarding refusal to supply contributed to the decision to withdraw the claim.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Highlights risks in collective actions where key third-party witnesses are unavailable.
Practice note: Demonstrates that tribunals will strictly scrutinize evidence of refusal to supply and market conduct.
What to monitor: Future procedural approaches to evidentiary gaps in SEP-related competition claims.

items.title: Asus signs up to Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool, ending litigation
items.title_zh: 华硕加入Sisvel Wi-Fi专利池结束诉讼
items.title_en: Asus Licenses Sisvel Wi-Fi Pool, Ending Litigation with Huawei and Philips
items.summary: Asus and Sisvel pool members end litigation with licence to the standard essential patents in Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool.
items.ai_summary_zh: 华硕获得Sisvel Wi-Fi多模专利池许可，涵盖Wi-Fi 6和7标准必要专利。此举结束了华硕与华为、飞利浦及Wilus之间的未决专利诉讼。Sisvel未披露财务条款。此前，慕尼黑地区法院在Wilus诉华硕案中澄清了FRAND程序方法，表示将更严格审查FRAND报价的合规性。
items.ai_summary_en: Asus has secured a license to the standard essential patents within Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool, covering Wi-Fi 6 and Wi-Fi 7 technologies. This agreement resolves pending patent litigation between the electronics manufacturer and pool members Huawei, Philips, and Wilus. The deal adds Asus to a roster of existing licensees that includes Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Panasonic, Sony Group Corporation, and ZTE. While Sisvel confirmed the resolution of disputes in venues such as the Munich Regional Court, the financial terms of the licensing arrangement remain undisclosed.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This settlement highlights the continued effectiveness of patent pools in resolving multi-jurisdictional SEP disputes without public disclosure of financial terms. For rights holders and implementers, the timing of this deal coincides with evolving judicial scrutiny regarding FRAND offer compliance in key European venues. The resolution suggests that major manufacturers are increasingly opting for pool licenses to mitigate litigation risks associated with fragmented enforcement actions across national courts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["华硕获Sisvel Wi-Fi多模专利池许可，涵盖Wi-Fi 6/7 SEP，结束与华…", "Sisvel未披露协议财务条款，华硕加入HPE、华为等现有被许可人名单", "慕尼黑地区法院在相关案件中明确将更严格审查FRAND报价的合规性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Asus obtained a license for Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool, covering SEPs for Wi-Fi 6 and Wi-Fi 7 standards.", "The agreement terminates ongoing patent disputes between Asus and pool members Huawei, Philips, and Wilus.", "Munich Regional Court recently clarified its approach to examining FRAND compliance in related proceedings."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor how joining established pools impacts litigation risk management for SEP implementers in Europe.", "Practice note: Note the Munich Regional Court's stated intent to closely examine FRAND compliance in future judgments.", "What to monitor: Track whether other major electronics manufacturers follow suit by licensing Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode program."]
core_points_en: Asus obtained a license for Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool, covering SEPs for Wi-Fi 6 and Wi-Fi 7 standards.
The agreement terminates ongoing patent disputes between Asus and pool members Huawei, Philips, and Wilus.
Munich Regional Court recently clarified its approach to examining FRAND compliance in related proceedings.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor how joining established pools impacts litigation risk management for SEP implementers in Europe.
Practice note: Note the Munich Regional Court's stated intent to closely examine FRAND compliance in future judgments.
What to monitor: Track whether other major electronics manufacturers follow suit by licensing Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode program.

items.title: Nokia cannot include cross-licence requirement in Acer/Asus arbitration offers
items.title_zh: 英上诉法院裁定：诺基亚不得在仲裁要约中强制要求交叉许可
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Bars Nokia from Including Cross-Licence Requirement in Arbitration Offers
items.summary: The English Court of Appeal ruling has implications for parallel disputes between the implementers and Nokia’s customers
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文仅显示英国上诉法院作出一项裁决，认定诺基亚（Nokia）不能在针对宏碁（Acer）和华硕（Asus）的仲裁要约中包含交叉许可要求。该裁决对实施者与诺基亚客户之间的平行争议具有潜在影响。由于正文材料极度匮乏，无法提供案件细节、法律依据或具体判决理由。
items.ai_summary_en: The English Court of Appeal has ruled that Nokia cannot include a cross-licence requirement in its arbitration offers to Acer and Asus. This decision addresses the permissibility of specific licensing conditions within arbitration frameworks. The ruling is noted to have implications for parallel disputes involving implementers and Nokia's customers, potentially influencing how similar licensing negotiations are structured in future SEP-related proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note: 需关注该判例是否确立SEP许可谈判中交叉许可条款的法律边界。What to monitor: 后续是否有更多实施者援引此判例挑战类似许可条件，以及其对全球FRAND费率确定的潜在辐射效应。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment clarifies the boundaries of acceptable terms within arbitration offers for standard essential patents. Rights holders and implementers should note the judicial stance on linking arbitration participation with cross-licensing obligations. The outcome may influence negotiation dynamics in ongoing and future SEP licensing discussions across European jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["标题显示英国上诉法院裁定诺基亚不得在仲裁要约中加入交叉许可要求", "原文摘要显示该裁决将影响实施者与诺基亚客户间的平行争议"]

items.title: Technology licensing under the revised TTBER: clearer guidance for pools, FRAND and collective negotiations
items.title_zh: 修订版TTBER下技术许可：专利池、FRAND及集体谈判指南更清晰
items.title_en: EU Revised TTBER: Enhanced Guidance on Pools, FRAND and Collective Negotiations
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会于2026年4月16日通过修订版《技术转移集体豁免条例》(TTBER)及配套指南，将于5月1日生效。新规保留原有安全港结构，但细化了针对专利池、标准必要技术(SEP)许可及集体谈判的合规指导。对于涉及复杂许可模式的企业，需重点评估协议结构以降低反垄断风险，确保符合TFEU第101(3)条要求。
items.ai_summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the European Commission adopted the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation (TTBER) and accompanying Guidelines, effective 1 May 2026. The new framework replaces the 2014 rules, maintaining the core Article 101(3) TFEU safe harbour structure while providing detailed guidance on modern licensing practices. Key updates address technology pools, licensing negotiation groups (LNGs), data licensing, and standard-essential technology licensing. For rights holders and licensees, compliance now requires careful structuring of complex agreements to mitigate competition law risks beyond mere market share thresholds.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注新规对SEP许可及专利池的合规要求，特别是在参与集体谈判时。建议重新审查现有许可协议，确保不包含核心限制条款，并利用新指南优化FRAND谈判策略，以降低在欧面临的反垄断调查与诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: The updated framework signals that the European Commission expects parties to actively manage competition law risks in sophisticated licensing structures. While the basic block exemption mechanics remain unchanged, the detailed guidance on pools and collective negotiations means that standard template agreements may no longer suffice for high-value or multi-party arrangements. Rights holders should assess whether their current portfolio licences and negotiation protocols align with the new interpretative guidance to ensure continued eligibility for the safe harbour.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["修订版TTBER及指南于2026年5月1日生效，取代2014年框架", "新增对专利池、数据许可及标准必要技术许可的详细竞争法指导", "企业需优化复杂许可协议结构以规避反垄断风险并享受安全港保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The revised TTBER retains the familiar safe harbour for technology transfer agreements below market share thresholds without hardcore restrictions.", "New Guidelines provide specific competition law clarity for technology pools, licensing negotiation groups, and standard-essential technology licensing.", "Complex licensing models involving data rights or collective negotiations require structured documentation to reduce antitrust exposure."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重新审查SEP许可协议以符合新规反垄断合规要求", "利用新指南优化专利池参与及集体谈判策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies using patent pools or engaging in collective licensing must review agreement structures against the new Guidelines to maintain safe harbour protection.", "Practice note: Drafting teams should incorporate specific compliance checks for LNGs and SEP licensing clauses to address the Commission's updated expectations on competition risk.", "What to monitor: Future enforcement decisions by the EC regarding technology pools and collective negotiation groups will clarify the practical boundaries of the revised Guideline…"]
core_points_en: The revised TTBER retains the familiar safe harbour for technology transfer agreements below market share thresholds without hardcore restrictions.
New Guidelines provide specific competition law clarity for technology pools, licensing negotiation groups, and standard-essential technology licensing.
Complex licensing models involving data rights or collective negotiations require structured documentation to reduce antitrust exposure.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies using patent pools or engaging in collective licensing must review agreement structures against the new Guidelines to maintain safe harbour protection.
Practice note: Drafting teams should incorporate specific compliance checks for LNGs and SEP licensing clauses to address the Commission's updated expectations on competition risk.
What to monitor: Future enforcement decisions by the EC regarding technology pools and collective negotiation groups will clarify the practical boundaries of the revised Guideline…

items.title: LD The Hague, May 11, 2026, Order, UPC-CFI_478/2025, UPC_CFI_585/2026
items.title_zh: UPC海牙分部：证据保全令需满足四项累积条件
items.title_en: UPC Hague: Evidence Production Orders Require Cumulative Conditions and Proportionality
items.summary: Under R. 190 RoP and Art. 59(1) UPCA, evidence production orders require four cumulative conditions: plausible evidence, specified evidence within the other party’s control, confidentiality protection, and proportionality
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC海牙地方法院裁定，依据《程序规则》第190条及《统一专利法院协定》第59(1)条，发布证据保全令需满足四项累积条件：初步证据、对方控制下的特定证据、保密保护及比例原则。法院援引Winnow v Orbisk等先例，指出已授权欧洲专利具有有效性推定，除非明显无效。鉴于侵权验证需物理测试，法院批准获取部分产品样本及营销材料，但基于比例原则缩减了样本数量。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in The Hague clarified the requirements for evidence production orders under Rule 190 of the Rules of Procedure and Article 59(1) of the Unified Patent Court Agreement. The Court established that four cumulative conditions must be met: plausible evidence, specified evidence within the opposing party's control, confidentiality protection, and proportionality. Citing Winnow v Orbisk and Oppo v Panasonic, the judgment affirmed that granted European patents carry a presumption of validity, which is only displaced by clear-cut invalidity. The Court reduced the requested physical samples from six to two variants based on proportionality, while upholding the quantity needed for ballistic testing where infringement verification depends on direct physical analysis rather than mathematical extrapolation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC在证据保全阶段的审查标准，强调了对已授权专利有效性的尊重及比例原则的适用。实务中，申请人需确保证据请求的具体性与必要性，避免过度索取。对于依赖物理测试认定侵权的案件，提前规划测试方案并论证样本获取的必要性至关重要。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision reinforces the structured approach of the UPC towards pre-trial evidence gathering, balancing the rights holder's need to verify infringement against the defendant's interest in avoiding disproportionate burdens. It confirms that while patent validity is presumed, the specificity and necessity of requested evidence remain subject to rigorous judicial scrutiny. For practitioners, the ruling highlights that requests for physical products must be tightly scoped to what is strictly necessary for technical verification, particularly when infringement claims rely on empirical testing outcomes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["证据保全令需满足初步证据、特定性、保密性及比例原则四项累积条件", "已授权欧洲专利享有有效性推定，仅明显无效情形可推翻该推定", "为验证依赖测试的侵权，可下令提供物理样本，但需符合比例原则限制范围"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 190 RoP and Article 59(1) UPCA require plausible evidence, specific controlled evidence, confidentiality, and proportionality.", "Granted European patents benefit from a validity presumption unless a clear-cut case of invalidity is demonstrated at this stage.", "Physical samples may be ordered for necessary testing, but requests are strictly limited by proportionality principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 明确UPC证据保全门槛，指导专利维权策略", "Practice note/实务观察: 论证样本获取必要性时需结合具体测试需求与比例原则", "What to monitor/后续观察: 关注UPC对“明显无效”标准的进一步界定及…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Clarifies the high threshold for obtaining evidence production orders, requiring precise justification of necessity and proportionality.", "Practice note: When seeking physical samples for test-dependent infringement, limit requests to the minimum viable set to satisfy proportionality requirements.", "What to monitor: How the UPC further defines 'clear-cut' invalidity cases that might displace the presumption of validity during evidence stages."]
core_points_en: Rule 190 RoP and Article 59(1) UPCA require plausible evidence, specific controlled evidence, confidentiality, and proportionality.
Granted European patents benefit from a validity presumption unless a clear-cut case of invalidity is demonstrated at this stage.
Physical samples may be ordered for necessary testing, but requests are strictly limited by proportionality principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Clarifies the high threshold for obtaining evidence production orders, requiring precise justification of necessity and proportionality.
Practice note: When seeking physical samples for test-dependent infringement, limit requests to the minimum viable set to satisfy proportionality requirements.
What to monitor: How the UPC further defines 'clear-cut' invalidity cases that might displace the presumption of validity during evidence stages.

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 12, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1747/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞：临时措施中申请人需充分证明侵权可能性
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Strict Proof Standards for Infringement in Provisional Measures
items.summary: The applicant bears the burden of proof for infringement in provisional measures and must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not (Art. 62 UPCA, R. 211 RoP)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院裁定，在临时措施程序中，申请人承担证明侵权的举证责任，且须以足够确定性证明侵权可能性大于非侵权。法院认定仅凭被告网站和宣传材料不足以建立复杂技术领域的侵权证据链，因未针对单一产品进行特征比对。由于侵权未成立，法院无需审查专利有效性及紧迫性等其他要件。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf dismissed a request for provisional measures, emphasizing that applicants bear the burden of proving infringement with sufficient certainty under Article 62 UPCA and Rule 211 RoP. The Court ruled that promotional materials alone are insufficient for complex technologies requiring feature-by-feature analysis. Furthermore, method claims construed as relating to a single product require all features to be present in one appliance; distinct treatment phases using different aligners cannot be combined to establish infringement. Speculative reasoning failed to meet the required standard of proof.
items.ai_insight_zh: 本案明确了UPC临时措施中较高的举证门槛，特别是在多阶段治疗等复杂技术场景中。权利人若仅依赖公开宣传材料而缺乏独立技术分析，极易因证据不足被驳回。实务中需注重对单一实物产品的特征比对分析。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the high evidentiary threshold for obtaining provisional measures at the UPC, particularly in multi-stage technical processes like clear aligner therapy. Rights holders must conduct independent technical assessments of specific accused products rather than relying on general marketing claims. The ruling reinforces that infringement analysis must strictly adhere to the single-product requirement when claims are construed accordingly, preventing the aggregation of features across different implementation stages.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["申请人须在临时措施中以足够确定性证明侵权可能性大于非侵权，承担主要举证责任", "对于单一产品权利要求，所有特征必须在单个器具中实现，不同阶段不能组合认定侵权", "仅依赖宣传材料和推测性证言无法满足复杂技术领域临时措施的严格举证标准"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applicants must demonstrate infringement is more likely than not, bearing the full burden of presentation and proof for facts establishing infringement.", "All claim features must be realized in a single appliance where the patent relates to a single product; separate phases cannot be combined.", "Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are inadequate evidence for provisional measures in technically complex fields."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性: 关注UPC临时禁令申请中的证据准备标准…", "Practice note/实务观察: 复杂技术案件需进行细致的单产品特征比对，而非组…", "What to monitor/后续观察: UPC其他分部是否跟进此严格的举证责任解释…"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders seeking provisional measures must secure robust, product-specific technical evidence rather than relying on public promotional materials.", "Practice note: Ensure infringement arguments align with the single-appliance requirement for method claims, avoiding combination of features from different stages.", "What to monitor: Whether other UPC divisions adopt similarly strict interpretations regarding the sufficiency of evidence for complex, multi-stage technologies."]
core_points_en: Applicants must demonstrate infringement is more likely than not, bearing the full burden of presentation and proof for facts establishing infringement.
All claim features must be realized in a single appliance where the patent relates to a single product; separate phases cannot be combined.
Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are inadequate evidence for provisional measures in technically complex fields.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders seeking provisional measures must secure robust, product-specific technical evidence rather than relying on public promotional materials.
Practice note: Ensure infringement arguments align with the single-appliance requirement for method claims, avoiding combination of features from different stages.
What to monitor: Whether other UPC divisions adopt similarly strict interpretations regarding the sufficiency of evidence for complex, multi-stage technologies.

items.title: Video codec patent pool exclusive; US auto litigation data; landmark Nokia v Acer/Asus ruling; USPTO assignments data failure; precedential Magnolia Medical Technologies IPR decision; plus much more
items.title_zh: IAM周报：Nokia胜诉、UPC建议和解及VCL专利池变动
items.title_en: Nokia wins UK appeal; UPC recommends ZTE-Samsung settlement; VCL pool administrator vote
items.summary: Get ready for the new working week with a summary of all the stories posted on the IAM platform over the past seven days
items.ai_summary_zh: 本周IAM资讯涵盖多项欧洲知识产权动态。Nokia在英国上诉法院赢得针对Acer和Asus的标志性诉讼。ZTE与Samsung在统一专利法院（UPC）框架下，法院建议和解费率并作出FRAND认定。此外，VCL HEVC/VVC许可人正投票更换专利池管理人为Sisvel。其他内容涉及越南SEP执法、美国商业秘密法及特斯拉诉讼数据。
items.ai_summary_en: This weekly digest highlights key developments in global intellectual property. The English Court of Appeal issued a landmark ruling in Nokia v Acer/Asus, reinforcing judicial approaches to standard essential patents. In Europe, the Unified Patent Court recommended a settlement rate in the ZTE-Samsung dispute, while a German court made a FRAND determination. Additionally, licensors in the VCL HEVC/VVC patent pool are voting to appoint Sisvel as the new administrator. Other coverage includes US auto litigation trends and trade secret enforcement insights.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: These developments underscore the evolving landscape for standard essential patent licensing and enforcement across major jurisdictions. The UPC's involvement in recommending settlement rates highlights its growing role in coordinating cross-border disputes, while the UK ruling provides further clarity on appellate standards. Market participants should track administrative changes in major patent pools, as shifts in management can influence licensing frameworks and negotiation dynamics for implementers and rights holders alike.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Nokia在英国上诉法院赢得针对Acer/Asus的标志性专利诉讼判决", "UPC就ZTE与Samsung案件推荐和解费率，德国法院作出FRAND认定", "VCL HEVC/VVC许可人拟投票将专利池管理员变更为Sisvel"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The English Court of Appeal delivered a significant judgment in the Nokia v Acer/Asus dispute regarding patent rights.", "The Unified Patent Court recommended a settlement rate in the ZTE-Samsung case, with parallel FRAND findings in Germany.", "VCL HEVC/VVC licensors are currently voting to change the patent pool administrator to Sisvel."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["Client relevance/客户相关性", "Practice note/实务观察", "What to monitor/后续观察"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor how UPC recommendations and national FRAND determinations interact to shape global licensing strategies.", "Practice note: Track the outcome of the VCL pool administrator vote, as management changes may alter licensing terms and access procedures.", "What to monitor: Follow subsequent appeals or enforcement actions stemming from the Nokia v Acer/Asus decision for precedential value."]
core_points_en: The English Court of Appeal delivered a significant judgment in the Nokia v Acer/Asus dispute regarding patent rights.
The Unified Patent Court recommended a settlement rate in the ZTE-Samsung case, with parallel FRAND findings in Germany.
VCL HEVC/VVC licensors are currently voting to change the patent pool administrator to Sisvel.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor how UPC recommendations and national FRAND determinations interact to shape global licensing strategies.
Practice note: Track the outcome of the VCL pool administrator vote, as management changes may alter licensing terms and access procedures.
What to monitor: Follow subsequent appeals or enforcement actions stemming from the Nokia v Acer/Asus decision for precedential value.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 30, 2026, Order Rejecting Appeal As Inadmissible, UPC_CoA_1/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定驳回上诉：程序合规与费用担保标准
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Rejects Adobe Appeal on Cost Security, Upholds Strict Procedural Compliance
items.summary: The Court of Appeal verifies ex officio compliance with the time limits and rules governing the appeal. When deciding on the admissibility of an appeal, an order rejecting the appeal as...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在Adobe诉Keeex案中，裁定驳回上诉并确认程序规则需严格适用以保障法律确定性。法院依职权审查上诉期限，认定驳回裁定未超出请求范围。此外，法院重申原告与被告的相对财务状况不构成Rule 158下的费用担保考量因素。该判决强化了UPC程序规则的刚性，强调避免司法任意性，对后续涉及费用担保及程序合规的案件具有先例约束力。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal issued an order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible in proceedings between Adobe Inc. and Keeex SAS concerning cost security. The Court confirmed it verifies compliance with time limits and procedural rules ex officio. It held that rejecting an appeal as inadmissible does not exceed the scope of the claims. Furthermore, the Court reaffirmed that strict application of procedural rules ensures legal certainty and prevents arbitrary treatment. Crucially, the relative financial situation of the claimant compared to the defendant is not a valid criterion for assessing cost security under Rule 158 of the Rules of Procedure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，须严守程序期限，避免因技术性违规导致权利丧失。针对费用担保，不能仅凭双方财力差距抗辩，需准备实质性的胜诉可能性证据。建议加强内部IP合规流程，特别是跨境诉讼中的程序管理，以降低因程序失误带来的高额成本风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision reinforces the UPC's commitment to rigid procedural adherence to maintain legal certainty and equal treatment. For rights holders and defendants, it clarifies that financial arguments alone cannot override standard cost security requirements. Practitioners must ensure precise compliance with filing deadlines and procedural formalities, as the Court will actively enforce these rules without leniency based on party status or financial standing.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院依职权严格审查上诉期限与程序规则，确保法律确定性及非歧视原则", "驳回上诉作为不可受理裁定，不视为超出当事人诉讼请求的范围", "当事人相对财务状况不构成申请费用担保的法定依据，维持既往判例立场"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal conducts an ex officio assessment of admissibility, verifying strict compliance with time limits and procedural rules governing appeals.", "An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is deemed within the scope of the claims and does not violate procedural boundaries under the Rules of Procedure.", "The relative financial disparity between claimant and defendant does not constitute a valid criterion for cost security assessments under Rule 158."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严守UPC程序期限，规避因技术性违规导致的权利丧失风险", "费用担保抗辩需侧重胜诉可能性，而非单纯依赖财力对比"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Financial disparity between parties is insufficient to challenge cost security orders; substantive procedural compliance is paramount.", "Practice note: Ensure rigorous internal monitoring of appeal deadlines and procedural steps, as the Court reviews admissibility ex officio.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 regarding cost security criteria and any potential shifts in the Court's strict interpretation of procedural rules."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal conducts an ex officio assessment of admissibility, verifying strict compliance with time limits and procedural rules governing appeals.
An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is deemed within the scope of the claims and does not violate procedural boundaries under the Rules of Procedure.
The relative financial disparity between claimant and defendant does not constitute a valid criterion for cost security assessments under Rule 158.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Financial disparity between parties is insufficient to challenge cost security orders; substantive procedural compliance is paramount.
Practice note: Ensure rigorous internal monitoring of appeal deadlines and procedural steps, as the Court reviews admissibility ex officio.
What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 regarding cost security criteria and any potential shifts in the Court's strict interpretation of procedural rules.

items.title: Réplique de Ferrari F355 : une contrefaçon sanctionnée par la justice néerlandaise
items.title_zh: 荷兰法院裁定法拉利F355仿制车侵犯版权及商标
items.title_en: Dutch Court Rules Ferrari F355 Replica Infringes Copyright and Trademark Rights
items.summary: Le tribunal de La Haye a sanctionné la commercialisation d'une réplique de Ferrari F355 pour contrefaçon de marque et atteinte au droit d'auteur. Cette décision du 8 avril 2026 illustre la protection cumulative offerte...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月8日，海牙地方法院裁定一荷兰车商销售的丰田改装法拉利F355仿制车侵权。法院认定该车型整体设计具独创性，受版权保护；且使用法拉利Logo构成商标侵权。被告需停止销售、销毁车辆并赔偿。此案凸显了欧盟法域内版权与商标权的累积保护机制，即使存在细微差异，若整体印象相似仍可能被判侵权。
items.ai_summary_en: The District Court of The Hague ruled on April 8, 2026, that a Dutch car dealer infringed Ferrari's copyright and trademark rights by selling a Toyota-based replica marketed as a Ferrari F355. The court found the vehicle's overall design sufficiently original for copyright protection, dismissing minor technical differences as negligible given the identical overall impression. Additionally, the unauthorized use of Ferrari logos constituted EU trademark infringement. The defendant was ordered to cease sales, destroy the replica, pay legal costs exceeding €22,000, and provide further compensation. This judgment highlights the cumulative protection available under EU law for automotive designs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企出口欧洲需警惕外观设计的版权风险，不仅限于专利。改装或复刻经典车型时，即便更换核心部件，若整体视觉印象相似且使用原品牌标识，将面临高额赔偿及销毁风险。建议提前进行FTO分析，避免在营销中使用易混淆的品牌元素。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates the robust cumulative protection afforded to automotive designs in the Netherlands and broader EU jurisdiction. Rights holders can leverage both copyright and trademark laws to combat replicas that mimic the overall aesthetic of protected models, even if internal components differ. For market participants dealing in modified or classic cars, reliance on technical differences alone is insufficient to avoid liability if the visual identity remains confusingly similar to the original brand.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定法拉利F355整体设计具独创性，符合版权保护条件", "被告使用相同Logo销售仿制车，构成欧盟商标权直接侵权", "判决强调版权与商标累积保护，细微差异不免除侵权责任"]

items.title: EPO Enlarged Board Clarifies: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art, Even If Irreproducible
items.title_zh: EPO扩大上诉委员会：公开产品即便不可再现亦属现有技术
items.title_en: EPO Enlarged Board: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art Even If Irreproducible
items.summary: The European Patent Office's Enlarged Board has fundamentally altered the landscape of prior art assessment, establishing that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art regardless of whether they can be analyzed or reproduced. This landmark decision removes the long-standing reproducibility requirement, making market availability alone sufficient to destroy novelty and significantly raising the stakes for timely patent filings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年7月2日，EPO扩大上诉委员会在G1/23案中裁定，申请日前公开的产品构成现有技术，无论其成分是否可被分析或再现。该决定推翻了G1/92案确立的“可再现性”要求，认为仅市场可获得性即足以破坏新颖性。此举消除了长期存在的法律不确定性，显著提高了及时提交专利申请的重要性，任何上市前公开均可能使后续专利无效。
items.ai_summary_en: In decision G 1/23, the EPO Enlarged Board of Appeal ruled that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art under Article 54(2) EPC, regardless of whether their composition or internal structure can be analyzed or reproduced. This judgment departs from the earlier G 1/92 precedent, which required both public availability and reproducibility for a product to form part of the state of the art. The Enlarged Board found that maintaining a reproducibility requirement would lead to absurd results, as many materials rely on irreproducible starting elements. Consequently, market availability alone is now sufficient to destroy novelty, significantly expanding the scope of prior art.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕产品上市前的任何公开行为，包括展会展示或销售，因即使技术细节未完全披露，产品本身也可作为现有技术驳回专利。建议建立严格的保密与申请时间管理机制，确保在申请日前不公开核心技术，避免在新规下丧失新颖性。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling fundamentally alters the landscape of prior art assessment in Europe. Rights holders must recognize that any public disclosure, including sales or exhibitions, may invalidate subsequent patent applications if the product itself is considered prior art, irrespective of reverse-engineering capabilities. This heightens the importance of strict confidentiality measures and timely filing strategies to prevent inadvertent loss of novelty due to pre-filing market activities.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["推翻G1/92先例，取消现有技术认定中的“可再现性”硬性门槛", "确立市场公开即构成现有技术原则，无论内部结构能否被反向工程", "极大扩展现有技术范围，对专利新颖性审查及申请策略产生深远影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["G 1/23 establishes that public availability alone suffices for prior art status, removing the reproducibility requirement previously mandated by G 1/92.", "Products placed on the market before the filing date are prior art even if their internal structure or composition cannot be analyzed without undue burden.", "The decision expands the state of the art to include all publicly accessible products, impacting novelty assessments for chemical and material compositions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化申请前保密管理，严防产品上市导致新颖性丧失风险", "调整全球布局策略，优先在欧洲完成申请后再进行市场推广"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Market availability now definitively destroys novelty, requiring rigorous control over pre-filing disclosures and product launches.", "Practice note: Assess prior art risks based on physical availability rather than analytical accessibility when evaluating novelty against existing products.", "What to monitor: Future case law applying G 1/23 to specific technical fields and potential impacts on opposition and invalidation proceedings."]
core_points_en: G 1/23 establishes that public availability alone suffices for prior art status, removing the reproducibility requirement previously mandated by G 1/92.
Products placed on the market before the filing date are prior art even if their internal structure or composition cannot be analyzed without undue burden.
The decision expands the state of the art to include all publicly accessible products, impacting novelty assessments for chemical and material compositions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Market availability now definitively destroys novelty, requiring rigorous control over pre-filing disclosures and product launches.
Practice note: Assess prior art risks based on physical availability rather than analytical accessibility when evaluating novelty against existing products.
What to monitor: Future case law applying G 1/23 to specific technical fields and potential impacts on opposition and invalidation proceedings.

items.title: PMAC Advances Operations with New Procedural Rules and Launch of Case Management System
items.title_zh: UPC专利调解仲裁中心发布新规并上线案件管理系统
items.title_en: UPC Patent Mediation Centre Adopts Rules and Launches Case Management System
items.summary: The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has taken significant steps toward full operational readiness,
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）行政委员会于2026年4月正式通过专利调解和仲裁中心（PMAC）的程序规则，并于5月12日上线专用案件管理系统（CCMS）。PMAC旨在提供调解、仲裁等替代性争议解决服务，涵盖欧洲专利及标准必要专利（SEP）纠纷。此举标志着PMAC具备全面运营能力，为权利人提供了高效灵活的专利争议解决新途径，进一步完善了UPC生态系统。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) has achieved key operational milestones with the adoption of its procedural rules and the launch of its Case Management System. The Administrative Committee formally approved the framework on 24 April 2026, establishing the legal basis for offering mediation, arbitration, and expert determination services. These mechanisms cover European patents, Unitary Patents, and Standard Essential Patent disputes. The electronic Case Management System went live on 12 May 2026, enabling digital submission of ADR requests. Mediation services are scheduled to begin in late May 2026, with other services following later in the year.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉及专利纠纷时，可关注UPC-PMAC提供的调解与仲裁渠道。相比传统诉讼，ADR程序更具灵活性与保密性，有助于降低维权成本并加速SEP许可谈判进程。建议企业评估将部分非核心或复杂跨国争议转入PMAC处理，以优化全球知识产权战略布局。
items.ai_insight_en: The operational launch of the UPC’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre provides rights holders with structured alternative dispute resolution options alongside traditional litigation. This development is particularly relevant for managing complex patent portfolios and resolving high-stakes technical or licensing disputes efficiently. Parties should review the newly published procedural rules and fee schedules to understand the scope and requirements of these non-litigation pathways within the unified patent system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC行政委员会正式批准PMAC程序规则，奠定法律与运营基础", "PMAC案件管理系统正式上线，支持电子提交替代性争议解决请求", "服务范围涵盖欧洲专利及SEP/FRAND案件，提供多元ADR选项"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Administrative Committee adopted PMAC's procedural rules on 24 April 2026, creating the legal foundation for alternative dispute resolution services within the court ecosy…", "PMAC launched its dedicated Case Management System on 12 May 2026, allowing parties to electronically submit requests for mediation, arbitration, and expert determination proceedi…", "The Centre will handle disputes involving European and Unitary Patents, including cross-border conflicts and Standard Essential Patent matters, with mediation services commencing…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC的ADR机制可降低在欧专利诉讼成本并提升解决效率", "针对SEP/FRAND纠纷，可探索通过调解仲裁达成更优许可条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Offers a new avenue for resolving SEP/FRAND and cross-border patent disputes outside of full litigation, potentially enhancing flexibility in portfolio managemen…", "Practice note: Review the adopted procedural rules and CCMS guidance to prepare for electronic case submissions as mediation services commence in late May 2026.", "What to monitor: Track the rollout schedule for arbitration and expert determination services later in the year and any updates to applicable fee structures."]

PMAC launched its dedicated Case Management System on 12 May 2026, allowing parties to electronically submit requests for mediation, arbitration, and expert determination proceedi…
The Centre will handle disputes involving European and Unitary Patents, including cross-border conflicts and Standard Essential Patent matters, with mediation services commencing…

items.title: Judgment of the General Court in Case T-24/25 Les Éditions Albert René v EUIPO - Works 11 Michał Lubiński (Obelix)
items.title_zh: 普通法院撤销EUIPO决定：武器类“Obelix”商标应无效
items.title_en: General Court Annuls EUIPO Refusal to Invalidate Obelix Mark for Weapons and Explosives
items.summary: The General Court nullifies EUIPO’s refusal to invalidate the word mark Obelix for weapons, ammunition and explosives
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-24/25案中撤销EUIPO拒绝宣告“Obelix”武器商标无效的决定。出版商Les Éditions Albert René依据在先OBELIX商标及声誉受损申请无效，EUIPO曾以声誉证据不足为由驳回。法院指出EUIPO分析有误，未综合评估商标声誉及两商标间的联系。法院强调需全局考察在先商标显著性及公众联想，认定存在损害在先商标声誉的风险，支持了权利人的主张。
items.ai_summary_en: The General Court annulled an EUIPO decision refusing to invalidate the word mark Obelix for firearms, ammunition, and explosives. The applicant, Les Éditions Albert René, relied on its earlier OBELIX mark and alleged damage to its reputation. The Court found EUIPO’s assessment of the earlier mark’s reputation incomplete, noting it improperly disregarded evidence of use with the registered symbol and in association with Asterix. Furthermore, EUIPO failed to adequately assess the link between the marks and the risk of detriment to the earlier mark’s distinctive character or repute, despite differences in goods.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces that EU trademark offices must conduct a holistic assessment of reputation, considering cumulative evidence rather than isolated deficiencies. Rights holders asserting cross-class protection based on reputation should ensure their submissions clearly demonstrate how distinct elements contribute to overall market recognition. The case highlights the importance of properly contextualizing co-branded or associated signs when establishing independent distinctiveness and repute for specific terms within a larger franchise.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定EUIPO对在先商标声誉评估不完整，忽视关键使用证据", "强调需全局评估商标间联系，不能仅因商品差异大而否定关联", "撤销EUIPO决定，支持基于声誉受损宣告争议商标无效的诉求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.", "Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.", "The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免注册知名文化符号，防范跨类声誉侵权法律风险", "重视商标使用证据留存，积极应对欧盟商标无效宣告挑战"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.", "Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.", "What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks."]
core_points_en: EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.
Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.
The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.
Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.
What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks.

items.title: Central Division Paris accepts revocation action against expired patent
items.title_zh: UPC巴黎分部：过期专利撤销之诉若具法律利益仍属可受理
items.title_en: UPC Paris Confirms Admissibility of Revocation Actions Against Expired Patents
items.summary: Huntsman (Europe) BV and Huntsman Holland BV v BASF SE — Central Division Paris, 22 April 2026 (UPC_CFI_461/2025))
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC巴黎中央分部在Huntsman诉BASF案中裁定，即使涉案专利已过期，只要原告因追索过往侵权损害赔偿而保留合法法律利益，撤销诉讼即具可受理性。法院重申在创造性评估中不受专利自身技术问题表述限制，可独立界定客观技术问题。此外，辅助请求的审查仅限于针对修改后权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动沿用对授权权利要求的异议。
items.ai_summary_en: In Huntsman v BASF, the UPC Central Division Paris ruled that a revocation action remains admissible even if the patent expired before filing, provided the claimant retains a legitimate legal interest. The Court found such interest existed because the defendant had initiated evidence preservation and announced damages claims for alleged past infringement. Additionally, the judgment clarified that the Court is not bound by the objective technical problem stated in the patent when assessing inventive step. Procedurally, it held that objections against granted claims do not automatically apply to amended claims in auxiliary requests.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕UPC程序灵活性，即便专利过期，对手仍可借撤销之诉清除障碍以索赔。应对时须精准论证法律利益缺失，并在答复辅助请求时聚焦具体修改点，避免异议范围不当扩大，强化程序抗辩策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies critical procedural boundaries for portfolio management and litigation strategy under the UPC framework. Rights holders must recognize that patent expiry does not automatically shield against revocation if past infringement liabilities are being pursued. For defendants, the ruling offers a pathway to clear patents from the register to mitigate historical damages exposure. Furthermore, the strict separation of validity attacks between granted and amended claims requires precise drafting of defenses and amendments, preventing the automatic carryover of broad invalidity arguments during proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["过期专利撤销案若为追索过往侵权赔偿，因具备法律利益仍可被受理", "法院在创造性分析中有权独立界定客观技术问题，不受专利文本束缚", "辅助请求审查限于针对修改权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动继承异议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant faces ongoing infringement or damages claims, establishing a continuing legal interest.", "The Court may independently determine the objective technical problem for inventive step analysis, rather than relying solely on the patent's formulation.", "When reviewing auxiliary requests, the Court limits its assessment to specific invalidity attacks directed at the amended claims, excluding general objections."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕过期专利撤销风险，积极抗辩原告缺乏合法法律利益以阻断诉讼", "优化辅助请求答复策略，严格限定审查范围，防止无效理由不当扩展"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Patent owners should assess whether expired patents still pose litigation risks due to pending damages claims, as revocation actions remain viable tools for comp…", "Practice note: When responding to auxiliary requests, parties must tailor invalidity arguments specifically to the amended features, as prior objections to granted claims may not…", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions defining the scope of 'legitimate legal interest' post-expiry and how courts independently formulate technical problems in complex inventive…"]
core_points_en: Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant faces ongoing infringement or damages claims, establishing a continuing legal interest.
The Court may independently determine the objective technical problem for inventive step analysis, rather than relying solely on the patent's formulation.
When reviewing auxiliary requests, the Court limits its assessment to specific invalidity attacks directed at the amended claims, excluding general objections.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Patent owners should assess whether expired patents still pose litigation risks due to pending damages claims, as revocation actions remain viable tools for comp…
Practice note: When responding to auxiliary requests, parties must tailor invalidity arguments specifically to the amended features, as prior objections to granted claims may not…
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions defining the scope of 'legitimate legal interest' post-expiry and how courts independently formulate technical problems in complex inventive…

items.title: Mind the SEP: UKIPO signals upcoming SEP reform
items.title_zh: 英专局信号：即将推进标准必要专利改革
items.title_en: UKIPO Corporate Plan Signals Upcoming Standard Essential Patent Regulatory Reforms
items.summary: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, outlining its policy priorities for the coming…
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月11日，英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布2026-2027企业计划，明确将标准必要专利（SEP）作为政策重点。针对当前生态系统存在的许可不透明、必要性不确定及争议解决成本高企等结构性挑战，UKIPO计划在2026年完成政策制定并推动监管框架变革。此举旨在提升英国创新吸引力，虽无即时立法，但预示未来将在许可透明度、SEP必要性认定及纠纷效率方面进行改革。
items.ai_summary_en: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, identifying standard essential patents (SEPs) as a critical policy priority. The plan acknowledges longstanding structural challenges within the SEP ecosystem, specifically limited licensing transparency, uncertainty regarding patent essentiality, and costly dispute resolution mechanisms that disproportionately impact small and medium-sized enterprises. While no immediate legislative changes are introduced, the UKIPO intends to complete policy development in 2026, aiming to enhance regulatory frameworks to support the UK’s position as a leading jurisdiction for innovation and technology-driven industries.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信及科技企业在英面临更严格的SEP合规环境。建议密切关注UKIPO后续政策细则，提前评估现有许可协议的透明度与必要性条款风险。应加强内部SEP管理能力，积极参与行业咨询，以应对潜在的监管变化及更高的争议解决效率要求，避免在新一轮改革中处于被动地位。
items.ai_insight_en: This update signals a sustained regulatory focus on SEP governance in the UK, aligning with broader European efforts to streamline SEP licensing and enforcement. For rights holders and implementers, the emphasis on reducing complexity and cost suggests future frameworks may prioritize procedural efficiency and clarity over substantive FRAND rate-setting. Stakeholders should prepare for potential shifts in how essentiality is verified and how disputes are mediated, ensuring internal compliance structures are adaptable to upcoming policy refinements aimed at supporting SMEs and technological growth.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO将SEP列为核心政策领域，致力于解决许可不透明及必要性不确定性问题", "计划于2026年完成政策制定，推动监管框架改革以优化争议解决机制", "改革旨在降低中小企业成本，维持英国作为全球技术创新领先司法管辖区的吸引力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UKIPO identifies SEPs as vital to the UK economy but cites structural issues like opaque licensing practices and essentiality uncertainty.", "Policy development will continue throughout 2026 to address inefficiencies in dispute resolution and improve overall market transparency.", "Reforms aim to maintain the UK's attractiveness for innovation without introducing immediate legal changes or specific legislative proposals yet."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UKIPO后续政策细则，评估现有许可协议合规风险", "加强内部SEP管理，积极参与咨询以应对监管变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders and implementers must anticipate enhanced scrutiny on licensing transparency and essentiality declarations under the new framework.", "Practice note: Review current SEP management protocols to ensure readiness for potential regulatory changes focusing on dispute resolution efficiency.", "What to monitor: Watch for the UKIPO's completion of policy development work in 2026 and any subsequent consultations on regulatory adjustments."]
core_points_en: The UKIPO identifies SEPs as vital to the UK economy but cites structural issues like opaque licensing practices and essentiality uncertainty.
Policy development will continue throughout 2026 to address inefficiencies in dispute resolution and improve overall market transparency.
Reforms aim to maintain the UK's attractiveness for innovation without introducing immediate legal changes or specific legislative proposals yet.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and implementers must anticipate enhanced scrutiny on licensing transparency and essentiality declarations under the new framework.
Practice note: Review current SEP management protocols to ensure readiness for potential regulatory changes focusing on dispute resolution efficiency.
What to monitor: Watch for the UKIPO's completion of policy development work in 2026 and any subsequent consultations on regulatory adjustments.

items.title: PMAC's Case Management System now live
items.title_zh: PMAC案件管理系统正式上线
items.title_en: UPC's PMAC Launches Case Management System for Mediation and Arbitration
items.summary: As of today, 12 May 2026, the Case Management System of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) is live
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月12日，统一专利法院（UPC）宣布其专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）的案件管理系统（CCMS）正式启用。该系统支持用户在线提交替代性争议解决（ADR）信息及调解程序请求。PMAC网站已设立专门页面提供系统使用指南。此举标志着UPC在优化专利纠纷非诉讼解决机制方面迈出重要一步，为当事人提供了更便捷的调解与仲裁服务渠道，有助于提高争议解决效率并降低诉讼成本。
items.ai_summary_en: As of 12 May 2026, the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) has officially launched its Case Management System (CCMS). This digital platform enables parties to submit requests for ADR information sessions and mediation proceedings directly through the Centre. The PMAC website now features a dedicated section providing practical guidance on navigating the new system. This development marks the operational availability of structured alternative dispute resolution services under the Unified Patent Court framework, offering a streamlined digital interface for initiating non-litigious patent dispute resolutions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利纠纷时，应关注并利用PMAC的调解与仲裁机制。相比UPC诉讼，ADR程序通常更具成本效益且保密性强。建议企业熟悉CCMS操作流程，在争议初期评估通过调解快速和解的可能性，以规避高昂的跨国诉讼风险并维护商业关系。
items.ai_insight_en: The launch of the PMAC Case Management System provides rights holders with a formalized digital channel for accessing alternative dispute resolution mechanisms within the UPC ecosystem. For in-house counsel and IP teams managing European portfolios, this infrastructure supports efficient initiation of mediation or arbitration without immediate recourse to full litigation. Monitoring the uptake and procedural rules of this system will help stakeholders evaluate its utility for resolving cross-border patent disputes efficiently.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PMAC案件管理系统于2026年5月12日正式上线运行", "支持在线提交ADR信息会话及调解程序相关请求", "官网发布专用页面提供系统操作实务指导"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The PMAC Case Management System is live as of 12 May 2026, enabling online submission of ADR requests.", "Parties can now initiate ADR information sessions and formal mediation proceedings via the new digital portal.", "Practical user guidance for the Case Management System is available on the dedicated PMAC website page."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC调解机制可降低在欧专利维权成本", "熟悉CCMS系统以便高效启动替代性争议解决程序"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Provides a structured digital pathway for initiating mediation and arbitration under the UPC framework.", "Practice note: Review the dedicated PMAC guidance page to understand submission requirements for ADR requests.", "What to monitor: Track future updates on PMAC procedural rules and case statistics to assess ADR effectiveness."]
core_points_en: The PMAC Case Management System is live as of 12 May 2026, enabling online submission of ADR requests.
Parties can now initiate ADR information sessions and formal mediation proceedings via the new digital portal.
Practical user guidance for the Case Management System is available on the dedicated PMAC website page.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Provides a structured digital pathway for initiating mediation and arbitration under the UPC framework.
Practice note: Review the dedicated PMAC guidance page to understand submission requirements for ADR requests.
What to monitor: Track future updates on PMAC procedural rules and case statistics to assess ADR effectiveness.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-797/23 Meta Platforms Ireland (Fair compensation)
items.title_zh: CJEU：成员国可规定新闻出版者获在线使用公平报酬
items.title_en: CJEU: Member States May Provide Fair Remuneration for Press Publishers
items.summary: Member States may make provision for publishers of press publications to be entitled to fair remuneration when they grant online service providers authorisation to use those publications
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲法院在Meta诉意大利AGCOM案中裁定，成员国可立法赋予新闻出版者在授权在线服务提供者使用时获得公平报酬的权利。该权利符合欧盟法律，前提是报酬构成授权对价，且出版者可拒绝授权或免费授权。法院认为，要求平台谈判并提供数据虽限制经营自由，但有助于实现版权市场公平及保障出版业投资回收，平衡了各方权益。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C-797/23, the Court of Justice ruled that EU law permits Member States to establish systems granting press publishers fair remuneration when authorizing online service providers to use their publications. The judgment confirms this is consistent with the Digital Single Market framework, provided the payment constitutes consideration for authorization and publishers retain the right to refuse or grant free licenses. The Court further held that obligations requiring platforms to negotiate and provide data are justified restrictions on business freedom, necessary to balance intellectual property rights with media pluralism and ensure publishers can recoup investments in a transformed digital media landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海媒体及聚合平台需关注欧盟各成员国的国内法转化，特别是意大利等先行国家。应建立合规机制，评估是否触发“公平报酬”义务，避免侵权风险。同时，利用出版者可免费授权的条款，积极争取低成本内容合作，优化内容供应链成本结构。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment validates national legislative models aiming to secure revenue streams for press publishers from digital platforms. For online service providers, particularly those operating across multiple EU jurisdictions, the decision underscores the need to assess compliance with local 'fair compensation' regimes. It clarifies that while negotiation duties restrict operational freedom, they are legally defensible if structured to support a fair marketplace. Rights holders should note the explicit preservation of their ability to grant free licenses, which remains a key contractual lever in platform negotiations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["成员国可立法规定出版者就在线使用获公平报酬，符合欧盟法框架", "报酬须为授权对价，出版者有权拒绝授权或提供免费授权", "强制谈判义务旨在平衡经营自由与版权保护，支持出版业生存"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Member States may legislate for fair remuneration for press publishers authorizing online use by service providers.", "Remuneration must be consideration for authorization; publishers must retain rights to refuse or license freely.", "Negotiation and data provision duties are justified to balance business freedom with copyright protection objectives."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注成员国国内法转化，评估在线内容使用的报酬合规义务", "利用免费授权条款，争取低成本内容合作以优化供应链成本"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Online service providers must evaluate exposure to national fair remuneration schemes and prepare for mandatory negotiation frameworks.", "Practice note: Publishers should leverage the confirmed right to grant free licenses strategically during platform negotiations to maintain visibility.", "What to monitor: Implementation details of such remuneration systems in other Member States following the Italian model validated by the CJEU."]
core_points_en: Member States may legislate for fair remuneration for press publishers authorizing online use by service providers.
Remuneration must be consideration for authorization; publishers must retain rights to refuse or license freely.
Negotiation and data provision duties are justified to balance business freedom with copyright protection objectives.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Online service providers must evaluate exposure to national fair remuneration schemes and prepare for mandatory negotiation frameworks.
Practice note: Publishers should leverage the confirmed right to grant free licenses strategically during platform negotiations to maintain visibility.
What to monitor: Implementation details of such remuneration systems in other Member States following the Italian model validated by the CJEU.

items.title: La porcelaine de Limoges
items.title_zh: 利摩日瓷器获欧盟首项手工艺地理标志保护
items.title_en: Limoges Porcelain Becomes First French Artisanal GI Registered Under New EU Regulation
items.summary: La porcelaine de Limoges sgurtler@inpi.fr 11 mai 2026 Avec l'entrée en vigueur du règlement européen sur les indications géographiques pour les produits artisanaux et industriels le 1er décembre 2025, la porcelaine de Limoges devient ce 15 mai la première indication géographique protégée (IGP) française artisanale et industrielle à être enregistrée au niveau européen auprès de l'Office de l'Union européenne pour la propriété intellectuelle (EUIPO). Cette reconnaissance marque une étape décisive
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月15日，利摩日瓷器成为欧盟《手工业和工业产品地理标志条例》生效后，首个在欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）注册的法国手工艺及工业地理标志（IGP）。该认证基于其自2017年起获得的法国国家地理标志地位，通过简化程序转化而来。此举赋予其在欧盟27国的广泛保护及官方标识使用权，标志着法国传统工艺知识产权保护的重要里程碑，并为其他法国手工艺品的欧盟注册开辟路径。
items.ai_summary_en: On 15 May 2026, Limoges porcelain became the first French artisanal and industrial geographical indication (GI) registered at the EU level with the EUIPO. This registration follows the entry into force of the new EU regulation on GIs for craft and industrial products on 1 December 2025. Leveraging its national GI status granted by INPI in 2017, Limoges porcelain utilized a simplified transformation procedure without an opposition phase. The decision grants protection across all 27 EU member states and authorizes the use of the official 'Protected Geographical Indication' logo, marking a significant step for the protection of traditional French craftsmanship.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This registration demonstrates the operationalization of the new EU framework for craft and industrial geographical indications. For rights holders, it highlights the strategic value of securing national GI status as a foundation for streamlined EU-wide protection. The case illustrates how established regional brands can leverage existing national recognitions to achieve broader territorial coverage and enhanced enforcement capabilities within the single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["利摩日瓷器成欧盟新规下首个注册的手工艺地理标志", "凭借法国国家GI基础，经简化程序直接转化为欧盟IGP", "获欧盟全境保护权及官方标识使用权，强化品牌壁垒"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Limoges porcelain is the first French artisanal GI registered under the new EU regulation effective 1 December 2025.", "Registration used a simplified transformation from national INPI status, bypassing EU-level opposition procedures.", "The GI covers all production steps in Haute-Vienne, granting EU-wide protection and official logo usage rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟GI新规对陶瓷等手工艺品出口的合规冲击", "推动中国特色产品申请欧盟GI以提升国际品牌溢价"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders should assess if their national GIs qualify for simplified transformation to gain immediate EU-wide protection.", "Practice note: National GI recognition serves as a critical prerequisite for accessing the streamlined EU registration pathway.", "What to monitor: Future registrations of other French and European artisanal products under this new regulatory framework."]
core_points_en: Limoges porcelain is the first French artisanal GI registered under the new EU regulation effective 1 December 2025.
Registration used a simplified transformation from national INPI status, bypassing EU-level opposition procedures.
The GI covers all production steps in Haute-Vienne, granting EU-wide protection and official logo usage rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders should assess if their national GIs qualify for simplified transformation to gain immediate EU-wide protection.
Practice note: National GI recognition serves as a critical prerequisite for accessing the streamlined EU registration pathway.
What to monitor: Future registrations of other French and European artisanal products under this new regulatory framework.
